title: Angular Basic — Paper 2
description: Foundational Angular concepts: components, data binding, directives, component communication, lifecycle hooks, services, modules, template-driven forms, pipes, and CLI.

questions.text: What does the 'templateUrl' property in the @Component decorator specify?

options.text: The URL of an external CSS file
The path to the component's external HTML template file
The routing URL for the component
The base URL of the application

questions.text: What is two-way data binding in Angular and what syntax is used for it?

options.text: Binding from template to component using [( )]
Syncing data between component and template in both directions using [(ngModel)]
Binding component events to the template using (( ))

questions.text: What does the ngStyle directive allow you to do in Angular?

options.text: Apply a CSS class based on a condition
Dynamically set inline styles on an HTML element
Scope CSS to the component view

questions.text: What is EventEmitter used for in Angular?

options.text: To emit DOM events natively from the browser
To create custom events that a child component can emit to its parent
To listen for global application events

questions.text: In what order are the lifecycle hooks ngOnChanges, ngOnInit, and ngAfterViewInit called?
What happens when you add a service to the 'providers' array of a specific component's @Component decorator?

options.text: The service becomes a singleton shared across the entire application
The service becomes available only within the root module
A new instance of the service is created for that component and its children
The service is registered globally as a singleton

questions.text: Which module provides commonly used directives such as NgIf, NgFor, and NgSwitch in Angular?
In a template-driven form, how do you access the form's validity state in the template?

options.text: By checking the form element's 'valid' HTML attribute
By using a template reference variable assigned to ngForm and checking .valid
By importing FormControl and calling its isValid() method
By subscribing to a FormGroup observable

questions.text: Which Angular built-in pipe formats a date value in the template?
Which Angular CLI command generates a new component with the default files?
What is ViewEncapsulation in Angular components?

options.text: A security feature that hides component logic
A mechanism that controls how component CSS styles are scoped and applied
A method to encapsulate component data from the template
A way to prevent child components from rendering

questions.text: What is a template reference variable in Angular and how is it declared?

options.text: A TypeScript variable declared with the 'ref' keyword
A component property prefixed with $ sign
A variable declared in the template using #varName that references an element or directive
A variable created by @ViewChild in the component class

questions.text: In Angular's *ngFor directive, what does the 'trackBy' function do?

options.text: It sorts the items in the list
It tracks changes in the list by a unique identifier to improve rendering performance
It limits the number of items rendered

questions.text: What is ng-content used for in Angular?

options.text: To project content from a parent component into a child component's template
To declare the component's internal content
To generate dynamic templates at runtime

questions.text: Which lifecycle hook is called after Angular has fully initialized all content projected into the component via ng-content?
When a service is provided in a specific NgModule's providers array (not root), what is the scope of its instance?

options.text: It is shared as a singleton across the entire application
It is scoped to that module and components within it
A new instance is created for every injection point
It is only available in the root component

questions.text: In a standalone Angular component, how do you import a module or other standalone components for use in the template?

options.text: By adding them to the declarations array of the @Component decorator
By adding them to the imports array of the @Component decorator
By adding them to the @NgModule imports array
By using the ng-import directive in the template

questions.text: What HTML attribute is used to bind an input's value to a component property using two-way binding in template-driven forms?
Which pipe would you use to display the text 'hello world' as 'Hello World' in Angular?
Which Angular CLI command compiles the application and outputs production-ready files to the dist folder?
Which ViewEncapsulation mode makes component styles apply globally (no encapsulation)?
What does attribute binding allow you to do in Angular?

options.text: Bind a component property to a DOM property
Set HTML attributes that have no corresponding DOM property using [attr.name]
Bind event attributes to component methods
Create custom HTML attributes for styling

questions.text: What does the *ngSwitch directive set of directives include?
How does the parent component pass data to a child component in Angular?

options.text: By calling a method on the child component instance
By binding to the child component's @Input() property using property binding [inputName]="value"

questions.text: What interface should a component implement to use the ngOnInit lifecycle hook?
What is the purpose of the inject() function introduced in Angular 14?

options.text: It allows injecting dependencies outside of a constructor, such as in field initializers or factory functions
It creates a new injector instance
It injects HTML content into the template

questions.text: Which property of @NgModule lists the services that the module contributes to the global collection of services?
What does the 'touched' state represent in an Angular template-driven form control?

options.text: The input has been modified by the user
The input has been validated at least once
The user has focused and then blurred the input field

questions.text: How do you chain multiple pipes together in an Angular template?

options.text: By separating pipes with | character: value | pipe1 | pipe2
By using a comma: value, pipe1, pipe2

questions.text: What is the purpose of the angular.json file in an Angular project?

options.text: It contains workspace and project configuration for the Angular CLI
